participants.name: Eli Heidenreich
participants.notes: Combine invite (RB); measured 6-0, 198 at Combine. Weighed 202 at pro day (4 lbs heavier than Combine). Sat on Combine 40 of 4.44. Improved vertical by 0.5 inch over Combine mark of 35.5 (pro day vertical 35.0 reported; some sources indicate improvement). Combine: 4.44 forty, 35.5 vertical, 120 broad, 16 bench. Pro day 3-cone of 6.5 was the official sheet number but some stopwatches recorded faster. Did only receiver drills led by the NY Giants; was not asked to do RB drills. Military service deferred - eligible to play in NFL in 2026.

participants.name: Landon Robinson
participants.notes: Was not invited to NFL Combine; Shrine Bowl participant. Measured 5-11, 291 at pro day (2 lbs heavier than Shrine Bowl weight of 289). Arm length 31.625 and wingspan 76.75 per A to Z Sports. 40 time reported 'as fast as 4.78.' Was put through both defensive line and running back drills. Caught the ball extremely well in RB drills. 2025 AAC Defensive Player of the Year. First-Team All-American.

participants.name: Alex Tecza
participants.notes: Navy's starting running back in 2025 (158 carries, 902 yards, 11 TDs). Listed as participant per Pittsburgh Post-Gazette headline. No individual drill results found in available sources. High school teammate of Eli Heidenreich at Mt. Lebanon HS in Pittsburgh, PA. Listed at 6-0 per Navy athletics roster.

additional_notes: Navy's pro day was held outdoors in Annapolis, Maryland on Wednesday, March 5, 2026 — the first pro day of the 2026 season per NFL.com. The workout started at 4 PM, later than most pro days, as it could not begin before classes at the Naval Academy had ended. The temperature dropped drastically as the sun went down, making conditions cold by the time drills were run. Between 20-22 NFL teams attended (sources vary: 22 per Post-Gazette/NFL.com, 20 per A to Z Sports). Teams present included the Washington Commanders, Buffalo Bills, Green Bay Packers, Kansas City Chiefs, Chicago Bears, Minnesota Vikings, San Francisco 49ers, and Cincinnati Bengals, among others. PFN's pro day tracker listed only 2 tracked prospects for Navy. The A to Z Sports article referenced a participant list from Navy Football but the full list was not available in accessible search results. Coverage focused almost exclusively on Heidenreich and Robinson as the two primary draft prospects. Timing method is believed to be hand-timed given the outdoor setting and absence of electronic timing references in any source. Heidenreich's pro day vertical was reported as 35.0 inches by EssentiallySports (described as 'improved his vertical jump by one-half inch to 35 inches'), though his Combine vertical was 35.5 — this may reflect a reporting discrepancy (possibly improved from a Shrine Bowl mark, not the Combine mark). His Combine results (4.44 forty, 35.5 vertical, 120 broad, 16 bench) are not included in the pro day drill fields since he sat on those numbers.
